back_translation: And Balaam saw that it was good in the eyes of the LORD to bless Israel; and he did not go as time after time to seek omens; and he set his face toward the wilderness.
And Balaam lifted up his eyes, and saw Israel dwelling by tribes; and the Spirit of God was upon him.
And he lifted up his oracle and said: 'The utterance of Balaam son of Beor, and the utterance of the man whose eye is open.'
'The utterance of one who hears the words of God; who sees the vision of the Almighty — falling, and with eyes uncovered.'
'How good are your tents, O Jacob; your dwellings, O Israel!'
'Like valleys spread out, like gardens beside a river, like aloes the LORD has planted, like cedars beside the waters.'
'Water shall flow from his buckets, and his seed shall be in abundant waters; and his king shall be higher than Agag, and his kingdom shall be exalted.'
'God who brings him out from Egypt — like the horns of a wild ox to him; he shall eat the nations his enemies, and crush their bones, and pierce with his arrows.'
'He crouches, he lies down like a lion, and like a lioness — who shall rouse him? Those who bless you are blessed, and those who curse you are cursed.'
And the nose (anger) of Balak burned against Balaam, and he clapped his hands; and Balak said to Balaam: 'To curse my enemies I called you, and behold, you have blessed indeed these three times.'
'And now, flee for yourself to your place; I said: I will surely honor you, and behold, the LORD has withheld you from honor.'
And Balaam said to Balak: 'Did I not also to your messengers whom you sent to me speak, saying:'
'If Balak gave me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot transgress the mouth of the LORD to do good or evil from my own heart; what the LORD speaks — that I will speak.'
'And now, behold, I am going to my people; come, I will advise you what this people will do to your people in the latter days.'
And he lifted up his oracle and said: 'The utterance of Balaam son of Beor, and the utterance of the man whose eye is open.'
'The utterance of one who hears the words of God, and knows the knowledge of the Most High; who sees the vision of the Almighty — falling, and with eyes uncovered.'
'I see him, but not now; I behold him, but not near. A star treads forth from Jacob, and a scepter arises from Israel; and shall crush the corners of Moab, and tear down all the sons of Sheth.'
'And Edom shall be a possession, and Seir, his enemies, shall be a possession; and Israel shall do valiantly.'
'And one shall have dominion from Jacob, and shall destroy the remnant from the city.'
And he saw Amalek, and lifted up his oracle and said: 'First of the nations was Amalek, but his end is to perish.'
And he saw the Kenite, and lifted up his oracle and said: 'Strong is your dwelling, and set in the rock is your nest.'
'Nevertheless, Kain shall be for burning; how long until Asshur takes you captive?'
And he lifted up his oracle and said: 'Alas, who shall live when God appoints it?'
'And ships from the hand of Kittim, and they shall afflict Asshur, and shall afflict Eber; and he too shall perish forever.'
And Balaam arose, and went, and returned to his place; and Balak also went on his way.
